description: Specification for the per-participant pose keypoints shipped with the catalog. Keypoints use the 17-joint COCO Keypoint layout (the de-facto standard for 2D human pose), so the catalog's named positions can be consumed by any pipeline already built around COCO. Coordinates are IDEALISED, SCHEMATIC TEMPLATES — not motion-captured or image-derived. Each catalog participant references a base posture from the base-skeleton library and a placement transform (translate, scale, rotate, flip); the build step composes the final scene-space keypoints from those. This provides the symbolic-to-geometric bridge that named-position vocabularies (Wikidata, Wikipedia) lack and that keypoint datasets (COCO, MPII) provide only as unnamed coordinates.

keypoint_format.name: COCO-17
keypoint_format.citation: Lin T-Y et al. (2014). Microsoft COCO: Common Objects in Context. ECCV. Keypoint annotation format, CC-BY-4.0 annotations.
keypoint_format.coordinate_space: Normalised [0,1] x [0,1]; origin top-left; x rightward, y downward.
keypoint_format.coco_flat_encoding: Exported per participant as a flat array of 51 numbers: [x0,y0,v0, x1,y1,v1, ... x16,y16,v16] in joint_order.

placement_spec.description: A participant's scene keypoints = base_skeleton transformed by placement, then any joint_overrides. Applied in order: flip_x, scale, rotate_deg (about the skeleton centroid), translate, joint_overrides. Base skeletons are drawn in PROFILE view (v1.1.0): upright postures face +x and flip_x turns them to face -x; horizontal postures are drawn head-left and flip_x mirrors them head-right — so facing is legible in composed scenes.

fields.base_posture: id from the base-skeleton library (see keypoint-base-skeletons.json)
fields.translate: [dx, dy] in scene-normalised units, added after scaling/rotation
fields.scale: [sx, sy] multiplier on the local skeleton (default [1,1])
fields.rotate_deg: clockwise rotation in degrees about the skeleton centroid (default 0)
fields.flip_x: boolean; mirror left-right (swaps left/right joints). Use for facing direction (default false)
fields.joint_overrides: optional { joint_name: [x, y, v] } map of absolute scene-space values applied AFTER the transform — per-pose articulation the rigid transform can't express (wrapped legs, bracing arms)

dimensions.scale_citation: Canonical schematic skeletons defined in keypoint-base-skeletons.json (v1.0.0).
dimensions.description: The canonical body posture a participant is composed from. Scene arrangement is produced by the placement transform.

values.definition: Upright, weight on both feet. Profile, facing +x.
Upright torso, weight on the knees, shins back along the ground. Profile, facing +x.
Kneeling with the pelvis lowered onto the heels. Profile, facing +x.
Sitting on the ground, knees raised. Profile, facing +x.
Upright seat on a partner's lap, legs wrapped forward around them. Profile, facing +x.
Lying on the back, body horizontal, face up. Profile, head-left.
On the back with hips flexed, knees up and ankles raised. Profile, head-left.
Lying face down, body horizontal, forearms under. Profile, head-left.
Body inclined over a lying partner: arms extended to the ground, knees down, hips low. Profile, head-left.
On hands and knees, torso horizontal. Profile, facing +x.
Lying on one side, knees drawn forward in a shallow Z. Profile, head-left.
Kneeling straddle over a horizontal partner, torso upright, shins back. Profile, facing +x.
Shoulders and head on the ground, hips raised vertically, legs folding back. Profile.
Standing deep squat above a partner, knees strongly bent. Profile, facing +x.
On the back with hips lifted and back arched, feet planted (display arch/bridge). Profile, head-left.
Standing with the torso folded forward at the hips, hands toward the floor or a support. Profile, facing +x.
Held off the ground by a standing partner, torso upright, legs wrapped or held forward. Profile, facing +x.

references: Lin T-Y et al. (2014). Microsoft COCO: Common Objects in Context. ECCV.
COCO Keypoint Detection Task — 17-keypoint person layout.
